distribution.name: fallout_new_vegas_dataset.csv
distribution.description:
> We're different than other gangs, and not just because we dress better. We're not just a group of thugs looking for our next fight. The Kings are about an idea, you see? Where every man is free to follow his own path, do his own thing. Where every man is a king in his own right.
> - The King 🤯

## Overview
- Filename: fallout_new_vegas_dataset.csv
- Format: Comma-Separated Values (CSV)
- Encoding: Latin-1 (no BOM)
- Line endings: Unix-style (LF)
- Quote character: " (double-quote)
- Field separator: semicolon (;)
**NOTE:** header shows semicolon-delimited fields.

☢️ Rows with values: 59415

## Header (columns):

| Column | Description | Type |
|---|---|---|
| signature | Record signature / resource type (e.g., INFO, MESG, DIAL, NOTE, LSCR) | string |
| file_name | Origin file name (plugin/ESM/ESP) | string |
| formID | In-game form identifier (hex, 8-digit zero-padded) | string |
| editorID | Internal editor identifier | string |
| text_content | Main text / dialog / description (may include newlines) | string |
| content_source | Field or subrecord used as source (e.g., NAM1, DESC - Description, TNAM - Text / Topic) | string |
| name | Display name or record name  | string |
| topic | Dialogue topic type | string |
| quest | Associated quest reference (QSTI/ONAM), if any | string |
| topLevelPath | Logical asset path or hierarchy inside game archive | string |
| additional_content | Supplementary concatenated text (e.g., menu button text joined by pipe symbols | string |
| speaker | Speaking NPC | string |


- **Nullable / empty values:** empty fields appear as ;; or ;"";.

## Details on Signature entries
- **DIAL:** dialogue Topic records (the top‑level for lines of dialogue: **INFO records sit under these**).

- **INFO:** individual dialogue info entries *under a DIAL* (each line/response, with conditions, result scripts, speaker, etc.).

- **NOTE:** note records used for in-game readable notes/holotapes/misc “data” items (what appears in the Notes/Audio tabs in the Pip‑Boy).

- **MESG (Message records):** simple message boxes, notifications, and pop‑up texts used by scripts, quests, etc.

- **LSCR (Load Screen records):** define the loading screens (image, text, conditions) shown during area loads or fast travel.

## Recommended import settings (Pandas):
- delimiter=";"
- encoding="latin-1"
- dtype for formID/editorID as string to preserve leading zeros/hex
- contents may contain encoded newlines (\n) and pipe separators (|); preserve exact strings for NLP tasks.

## Data types & validation hints:
- signature, file_name, editorID, content_source, name, topic, quest, topLevelPath, additional_content, speaker: latin-1 strings.
- formID: hex or decimal string; validate pattern (^[0-9A-Fa-f]+$) if hex.
- text_content: Unicode text may include markup, escape sequences, or encoded quoting. Preserve exact text for NLP tasks.

Character encoding considerations: preserve diacritics and special characters; normalize to NFC if combining marks are present. 

## Data provenance & extraction notes
Extracted using a plugin-parsing exporter ([FNVEdit (xEdit build for New Vegas)](https://www.nexusmods.com/newvegas/mods/34703)) that walks game records (INFO, MESG, DIAL, NOTE, LSCR) and collects subrecords (NAM1 responses, ITXT menu buttons, TNAM text, DESC descriptions).

❄ Extraction date: January 2026 ☃

field.name: file_name
field.description: Plugin/esm filename (mod-name). Useful for filtering mod vs vanilla content.

field.name: formID
field.description: Canonical object id (hex).  Use as stable key.

field.name: text_content
field.description: Raw string content. Contains \n for encoded newlines and '|' between concatenated responses.

field.name: content_source
field.description: Original subfield (NAM1, DESC, FULL, TNAM). High cardinality values.

field.name: topLevelPath
field.description: PathName provides hierarchical context (asset location inside plugin).

field.name: additional_content
field.description: Menu button texts, concatenated with pipe symbol where present

name: Fallout New Vegas dataset
description:
![](https://www.googleapis.com/download/storage/v1/b/kaggle-user-content/o/inbox%2F27942110%2Faa05665df4531ba3e4e59ace4f8f2ba4%2FFallout-1.jpg?generation=1770582448727080&alt=media)
*O vault‑bred scribe of Mojave's dim-lit strand,
Thy shady sand‑library keeps caps and secrets in its hand.*

## Overview
This dataset is a structured export of in-game **textual assets** from Fallout: New Vegas produced by an [automated extractor](https://github.com/brooks-code/new-vegas-papyri/blob/main/FNV_dialogue_export.pas). It captures dialogue lines, response texts, menu/button labels, object and note descriptions, quest-linked strings, and metadata. The dataset is optimized for narrative analysis, game-NLP research, dialogue modeling, modding workflows, and LLM fine-tuning on characters and quest content.

## Why is this dataset useful ?
- **High-granularity provenance:** *file_name* and *topLevelPath* map each string back to the originating plugin/ESM for traceability.
- **Speaker & topic mapping:** *speaker, topic, and quest* fields enable persona-conditioned modeling, speaker-specific style transfer, and dialogue-act classification.
- **Multi-response capture:** `INFO` responses and `MESG` menu buttons are concatenated with '|' so you can reconstruct full conversation trees or branching responses.

## Example applications
- Fine-tune a small-to-medium LLM to emulate Fallout NPC voices using speaker + topic conditioning.
- Build a retrieval-augmented assistant that answers “quest lore” queries by matching quest and text_content.
- Conduct a stylometric analysis of faction or character speech patterns.
- Create a dataset of branching responses for training dialogue policy or interactive fiction models.
- Extract named entities (locations, factions, items) and link them to formIDs for knowledge-base construction.

```python
import pandas as pd
df = pd.read_csv('fallout_new_vegas_dataset.csv', sep=';', encoding='latin-1', dtype=str)
```
